name: Resolve AI
tagline: An autonomous AI site reliability engineer that detects, diagnoses, and fixes production software incidents in real time.
why: Founded by ex-Splunk observability execs, Resolve hit a $1B valuation in its Series A about 18 months after founding. The $125M round was led by Lightspeed with participation from Greylock (which led the prior $35M seed alongside Fei-Fei Li and Jeff Dean) and Unusual Ventures.

name: 7AI
tagline: A platform of 40+ autonomous AI agents that conduct enterprise security investigations at scale.
why: Founded by the Cybereason co-founders, 7AI raised what it calls the largest cybersecurity Series A in history ($130M) about 10 months after exiting stealth. The round was led by Index Ventures with participation from Greylock, CRV, Spark Capital, and Blackstone Innovations Investments.

name: Artisan
tagline: An all-in-one outbound sales platform powered by 'AI employees' like Ava, an autonomous BDR.
why: Artisan became widely known via its viral 'Stop Hiring Humans' campaign and raised a $25M Series A in April 2025 led by Glade Brook Capital, with Y Combinator, HubSpot Ventures, and Day One Ventures participating after an earlier $11.5M seed that included Sequoia. Total funding is about $46M.

name: Fable Security
tagline: An AI-native human risk management platform that delivers real-time, behavior-based security interventions to employees.
why: Founded by two early Abnormal AI team members, Fable launched out of stealth in July 2025 with $31M total across a Greylock-led $6.5M seed and a Redpoint Ventures-led $24.5M Series A. The female-led team targets the human layer of enterprise cybersecurity, backed by two top-tier funds.

name: Sandstone
founder: Nick Fleisher, Jarryd Strydom, Liam Germain
tagline: An AI-native platform for in-house legal teams that pulls in data from HR, sales, and other departments to keep counsel informed.
why: Sandstone launched with a $10M seed led by Sequoia Capital. CEO Nick Fleisher previously led the legal technology practice at McKinsey, and the company is positioning itself as the operating layer for AI-native legal departments. Sequoia's backing at public launch is a strong early signal.

name: Adaptive ML
founder: Julien Launay, Baptiste Pannier, Daniel Hesslow
tagline: An enterprise RLOps platform that lets companies customize and operate open-source LLMs with reinforcement learning.
why: Founded by the team behind the Falcon and BLOOM open-source models, formerly of Hugging Face, Adaptive ML raised a $20M seed led by Index Ventures with ICONIQ Capital and Databricks Ventures at roughly a $100M valuation. The founders' foundation-model pedigree is a standout signal.

name: LegalFly
founder: Ruben Miessen, Kasper Verbeeck, Dennis Montegnies, Gregory Vekemans
tagline: A secure legal AI workspace with agents that automate contract analysis, regulatory compliance, and document management.
why: Built by former product leaders from Tinder, LegalFly raised a EUR 15M+ Series A led by Notion Capital with participation from redalpine and Fortino Capital. The team's consumer-scale product background plus a security-first enterprise legal AI product gives it a differentiated angle.

name: Pit
founder: Adam Jafer, Filip Lindvall, Fredrik Hjelm, Anton Oberg, Fredrik Olovsson
tagline: An 'AI product team as a service' that learns how an enterprise operates and builds custom production software for its internal workflows.
why: Co-founded by the team behind European scooter giant Voi (plus ex-Klarna and iZettle engineers), Pit exited stealth in 2026 with a $16M seed led by a16z, with Lakestar and angels from OpenAI, Anthropic, and Revolut. Early customers include Voi, Kry, and Stena Recycling.

name: HealthEx
tagline: A consumer consent and preference platform that unlocks compliant access to health data for patients and health systems.
why: Led by General Catalyst with participation from Electric Capital. Founders are repeat operators: Agarwal (ex-UCSF, MyoKardia/BMS) and Raghavan (founding team and VP Eng at BlueJeans, acquired by Verizon), tackling the high-value health data interoperability and consent problem.

name: Radical Health
tagline: Building a general-purpose AI oncologist that personalizes cancer care for patients and clinicians.
why: Pre-seed led by Khosla Ventures with Protagonist, incubated through Entrepreneurs First. The company has exclusive access to large hospital datasets covering about 11.8 million patient journeys, positioning it strongly in precision-oncology AI.

name: TORTUS AI
tagline: An AI clinical co-worker (O.S.L.E.R.) that automates documentation and admin so doctors can spend more time with patients.
why: Seed round led by Khosla Ventures with Entrepreneur First and notable angels including former NHS Chair Lord David Prior. Founded by cardiologist Dr. Dom Pimenta and ML engineer Christopher Tan, TORTUS is rolling out across the UK's NHS.

name: Zingage
tagline: An AI care-delivery platform that automates scheduling, intake, documentation, and billing for home care agencies.
why: Seed round led by Bessemer Venture Partners with TQ Ventures, South Park Commons, and WndrCo. Founders are proven operators (Hunt sold a prior marketplace; Tian was at Ramp and TikTok), and the platform already coordinates about 50,000 caregivers across 400+ agencies.

name: Parallel
tagline: AI agents that operate directly on top of legacy hospital software to automate medical coding, billing, and admissions.
why: Series A led by Index Ventures, with prior backing from Y Combinator, Frst, and Hexa, plus angels including Mistral's Arthur Mensch. CEO Lafforgue is ex-Meta/McKinsey and CTO Rydahl founded Hublo (Europe's largest healthcare staffing platform); the company raised its Series A less than a year after seed.

name: Foundation Health
tagline: AI-powered infrastructure spanning pharmacy operations, care coordination, and direct-to-patient delivery.
why: Series A led by Define Ventures with Intermountain Ventures, Vanderbilt University, and BoxGroup. Founded by Umar Afridi, co-founder of pharmacy-fulfillment company Truepill, giving the team deep domain credibility in the large, under-digitized pharmacy operations market.

name: Daydream
founder: Julie Bornstein, Matt Fisher, Dan Cary, Lisa Yamner Green, Richard Kim
tagline: An AI-powered conversational search and discovery platform that reinvents how people shop for fashion online.
why: Led by Julie Bornstein, an e-commerce veteran (ex-COO of Stitch Fix, ex-Sephora and Nordstrom). It raised one of the largest consumer seed rounds of 2024 at $50M, co-led by Forerunner Ventures and Index Ventures, with GV and True Ventures participating.

name: Infinite Machine
tagline: Designing and building next-generation personal electric vehicles, starting with the cyberpunk-styled P1 urban scooter.
why: Reinventing personal mobility with bold industrial design and a passionate early following for its P1 scooter and Olto e-bike. Its $9.3M seed round was led by Andreessen Horowitz (American Dynamism), with backing from the founders of Reformation, Replit, and Hugging Face, plus F1 champion Nico Rosberg.

name: Sweatpals
tagline: A social fitness platform that helps people build real-life connection and community through in-person workouts.
why: Riding the booming social fitness and run-club trend, building a fast-growing community-driven network. Its $12M seed round was backed by Patron, a16z speedrun, and Kevin Hart's HartBeat Ventures, with participation from WndrCo, Pear, and Antler.

name: Sitch
tagline: An AI-powered matchmaking app that pairs human-style curation with LLMs to set users up on real dates instead of endless swiping.
why: One of the buzziest entrants in the AI-dating wave, founded by Nandini Mullaji (Stanford GSB, early Bumble India team) and Chad DePue (ex-Snapchat, Microsoft). The seed round was led by M13 with participation from a16z speedrun and a strong angel syndicate, totaling about $9M.

name: Claim
tagline: A social rewards network where Gen Z users discover brands, earn cash back, and share and trade rewards with friends.
why: Strong Gen Z traction by turning brand discovery and cash-back rewards into a social experience. Its $4M seed was led by Sequoia Capital (with Susa Ventures and BoxGroup in the pre-seed), and it followed with a $12M Series A led by VMG Technology.

name: Pogo
founder: Dom Wong, Oskar Melking, Shikhar Mohan
tagline: A consumer app that lets users earn money from their own data, now powering an AI consumer-research platform built on purchase-verified buyers.
why: Pogo has amassed 3M+ opted-in users and reportedly delivers seven-figure revenue per employee. It has raised about $32M to date from a top-tier roster including Harry Stebbings' 20VC, Josh Buckley's Buckley Ventures, Mantis (The Chainsmokers), and the founders of Honey.

name: Growl
tagline: An AI-powered, wall-mounted boxing bag that projects a life-sized virtual coach for interactive at-home fitness.
why: Growl raised a $4.75M seed led by Skip Capital with Kima Ventures, Teampact Ventures, and UFC champion Ciryl Gane, positioning itself as the 'Peloton of boxing.' Its founding team includes talent from Peloton, Tonal, and Aescape, and an oversubscribed pre-order campaign signals early consumer traction.

name: Peripheral Labs
tagline: An AI neural-rendering platform that turns standard broadcast feeds into navigable 3D volumetric replays in near real-time.
why: Peripheral Labs raised a $3.6M seed led by Khosla Ventures with Daybreak Capital, Entrepreneurs First, and Transpose Platform. The founders led the University of Toronto's self-driving racing teams and repurposed autonomous-vehicle sensor tech for sports.

name: Fanstake
founder: Greg Glass, Donnie Flood, Alex Boisvert
tagline: A fan-powered NIL marketplace where college sports fans pledge money to recruit and retain athletes, paying only if the athlete commits.
why: Fanstake closed a $6.25M seed led by Courtside Ventures with Susa Ventures, Will Ventures, Scrum Ventures, and Alumni Ventures, drawing two top sports-focused VC firms into one round. It targets the fast-growing NIL economy and partnered with The Collective Association.

name: Uplift Labs
founder: Sukemasa Kabayama, Jonathan Wills, Rahul Rajan
tagline: AI-powered markerless 3D motion capture that replaces expensive lab setups with just a smartphone for athlete performance and injury prevention.
why: Uplift Labs has raised over $8.5M from investors including SoftBank's Deepcore, Stadia Ventures, and athlete-angels such as NBA's Seth Curry and Marc Gasol. Led by ex-Tesla Japan president Masa Kabayama, it scaled from 12,000 to nearly 20,000 athletes in 2025 and serves MLB, NBA, and NCAA teams plus ESPN.

name: Hexis
tagline: An AI-driven athlete nutrition app that delivers personalized carb-coded fueling plans that adapt in real time to training load.
why: Hexis raised GBP 1.6M in pre-seed funding from athlete-backed VC firm APEX and global sports investor Sport Republic, with Olympic medallists and pro cyclists participating. Founded by sports-science PhDs David Dunne and Sam Impey, it applies peer-reviewed research to personalized performance nutrition.

name: ScorePlay
tagline: AI-powered media asset management platform that helps sports organizations centralize, tag, and distribute their content.
why: Used by 200+ leagues, federations and teams across the NBA, Premier League, LaLiga and NHL. Raised a $13M (EUR 12.5M) Series A in early 2025 with FIBA backing, and counts Alexis Ohanian as both a customer and investor. Co-founders were featured on Forbes 30 Under 30.

name: Jump
founder: Marc Lore, Alex Rodriguez, Jordy Leiser
tagline: Unified fan-experience and ticketing platform, described as the 'Shopify for sports teams and venues.'
why: Gives professional sports teams full control of ticketing, content, commerce and gameday operations in one platform. Raised $25M in Series A funding in October 2025, backed by Alexis Ohanian's Seven Seven Six, Courtside Ventures and Forerunner. Founding team includes high-profile operators Marc Lore and Alex Rodriguez.

name: Surfe
tagline: Connected revenue workspace that syncs LinkedIn contacts and data directly into CRMs like Salesforce and HubSpot.
why: Bootstrapped to 1,500+ paying clients and EUR 1M ARR with no dedicated sales team before raising a EUR 4M seed. Used by revenue teams at Google and Uber, with strong product-led growth originating from a browser extension.

name: Vesence
tagline: AI agents that review law firms' documents, emails and projects in Microsoft Office before they are shared with clients.
why: Y Combinator-backed legal tech that raised a $9M seed in 2025. Targets the high-value, error-sensitive legal review workflow with AI agents embedded directly in Office. Featured in Sifted's B2B SaaS Rising 100.

name: Gradient Labs
founder: Dimitri Masin, Neal Lathia, Danai Antoniou
tagline: AI customer-support agent ('Otto') purpose-built for regulated industries such as financial services.
why: Founded by ex-Monzo data and ML leaders; scaled to GBP 1M ARR in three months. Doubled its Series A to $26M in 2025 as it expanded into the US. Its focus on safe, compliant AI for regulated operations is a clear differentiator.

name: Logic
tagline: AI-driven automation platform that lets business teams automate recurring review and decision processes without writing code.
why: Raised a $4.3M seed in October 2025, less than a year after founding. Targets no-code automation of repetitive decision workflows, a fast-growing category as foundation models lower the barrier to internal automation.

name: Wabi
tagline: A social platform, described as 'YouTube for apps,' where anyone can use prompts to instantly create and share mini-apps.
why: Founded by Replika creator Eugenia Kuyda, who has a track record building viral consumer AI. Raised an unusually large $20M pre-seed in November 2025, signaling strong investor conviction in consumer 'vibe-coding' platforms.

name: Aeon
tagline: AI-powered preventive health platform combining whole-body MRI, blood biomarker analysis and genetic testing for early disease detection.
why: Raised an EUR 8.2M seed in June 2025 led by Concentric. CEO Tim Seithe previously built and sold Tillhub to Unzer. Tackles preventable deaths through early detection, addressing a large and fast-growing preventive-health market.

name: Hormona
tagline: Femtech app and at-home testing that lets women track, understand and optimize their hormones.
why: Raised a GBP 5M seed in May 2025, co-led by Supernode Global and Voima Ventures. Award-winning app addressing an underserved women's health market, with at-home hormone testing and cycle forecasting. Earlier rounds were backed by Techstars and SFC Capital.

name: Peec AI
tagline: Analytics platform that helps brands track and improve how they appear in AI answer engines like ChatGPT.
why: As consumers shift from Google to ChatGPT, Peec AI helps brands monitor and optimize their visibility in LLM answers, a brand-new generative-engine-optimization category. Raised a $21M Series A led by Singular just four months after its seed round, one of the fastest follow-ons in the space.

name: Dazzle
tagline: Consumer tech startup from former Yahoo CEO Marissa Mayer focused on AI-powered consumer products.
why: Raised an $8M seed at a $35M valuation led by Forerunner's Kirsten Green, with Kleiner Perkins and Greycroft participating. Backed by top-tier consumer investors and led by a high-profile founder with deep consumer-product experience.
